normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit preconditions are stated in the provided Rust reference implementation (the snippet placeholder contains no code to extract requirements from).

The Rust reference implementation content was not provided (it appears as a placeholder `{{rust_reference}}`), so the exact computed function cannot be derived. This obligation represents the need to state the intended input/output relation once the actual code is available.

No termination argument can be extracted without the actual Rust code. This obligation represents proving that the algorithm terminates (e.g., via structural recursion or a decreasing measure) once the code is known.
No loop/recursion invariants can be extracted without the actual Rust code. This obligation represents identifying and proving the key invariant(s) that imply correctness.

No postcondition can be extracted without the actual Rust code. This obligation represents proving that the returned result satisfies the specification derived from the algorithm once the code is available.
